summary: 4 silent skip-pass sites -> in-body await assertDbAvailable() (TEST-FR-001). Helper async+awaited; lifecycle dbAvailable guards intact; B4 pure-unit untouched; no dead code; no prod code. No BLOCK/WARN.

findings.claim: Helper imported and used correctly; assertDbAvailable is async and awaited at all 4 sites
findings.evidence: requireDB.js:93 'async function assertDbAvailable'. Call sites quotaTOCTOU:169,268 and timeoutAbort:259,297 all use 'await assertDbAvailable()'. Throws [TEST-FR-001] on DB-down -> RED, not vacuous green.

findings.claim: Lifecycle dbAvailable guards intact; no ReferenceError; correct ordering
findings.evidence: let dbAvailable declared (quotaTOCTOU:113, timeoutAbort:227), set in beforeAll, still referenced by afterAll (if dbAvailable) and beforeEach (if !dbAvailable return). Only in-body guards removed. On DB-down, beforeEach early-returns (no DB call) then body assertDbAvailable() throws RED.

findings.claim: B4 pure-unit describe not touched / not made DB-dependent
findings.evidence: Lines 115-184 use mocked enqueue spy (mockEnqueueFn), no DB. File-level assertDbAvailable import (line 73) is unused by B4; adds no DB dependency. B4 stays Docker-free.

findings.claim: No leftover dead variables or unreachable code from guard removal
findings.evidence: dbAvailable still consumed by 3 hooks per suite. Removed console.warn skip blocks left no orphan. beforeEach comment accurate: DB-down throws in-body before any DB call in the body.
git diff --name-only returns exactly the 2 named test files; git diff --cached empty.

refers.why: test-quality judgments (assertion strength, mutation-kill, CI-RED confirmation) are zoe's column, not ernie's
security column owner; none triggered by this diff
